title: aster lanceolatus var. simplex
intro: Aster lanceolatus var. simplex, also known as the White Rod or White Aster, is a perennial plant in the daisy family, characterized by its simple leaves and white or pale pink flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Aster lanceolatus var. simplex include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as congestion and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and it can also be dried and used in capsules or added to salves and ointments for topical application.
